string.text: Keeps a SortingPlus inventory tidy: when an item lands out of order (gun modified, item moved, traded, marked favorite/junk) it snaps back into its sorted position in place, with no flicker and without losing your scroll position.
Master switch. When off, inventories behave like vanilla SortingPlus (items land at the end until you reopen the window).
When you mark an item favorite or junk (SortingPlus K / J keys or the right-click menu), snap it to the top / bottom instantly in place. When off, SortingPlus' own behavior is used.
Compatibility with the Looting Takes Time (Redux) mod, which reveals a looted body's items gradually. Only dead bodies are affected here; stashes and your own inventory always sort.
While Looting Takes Time is gradually revealing a looted body, do not re-sort it so the reveal is not disturbed. Leave on if you use Looting Takes Time.

ON: after the reveal finishes, snap the body into sorted order once (and keep it sorted as you loot).\nOFF: never sort a looted body - it stays exactly in the order Looting Takes Time placed it, even as you take items. (Requires "Defer while looting".)
Tint items you just took from a container, stash or body with a colored background, so you can see at a glance what you found. The highlight clears when you close the inventory.
Enable the new-loot highlight. Use the sliders below to choose the color.
Red component of the highlight color (0-255).
Green component of the highlight color (0-255).
Blue component of the highlight color (0-255).
Opacity of the highlight (0-255). Low values give a subtle tint; high values a strong, solid color. Default 60.

Anti-oscillation guard. Some items have an unstable sort key (e.g. a magazine while it is being bound) that can make a container re-sort every refresh and visibly churn. This detects that and briefly stops re-sorting so the list settles.
Back off from re-sorting a container that is churning. Leave on unless troubleshooting.
How many re-sorts of one container within a one-second window are allowed before the guard decides it is oscillating. Lower = more aggressive. Default 8.
How long the guard stops re-sorting an oscillating container so it can settle, then seamless sorting resumes. Default 1.5 s.
